heroDescription: Adhyatm Parivar is a vast organization dedicated to the welfare-oriented Jain Shasan for the world. The organization is committed to making every possible effort for religious and spiritual upliftment in society. So that every individual can elevate their life to the highest level, the organization undertakes the protection of ancient heritage as a support, organizes discourses, and conducts various rituals of Jain Shasan. Through this, values of morality, faith, and dedication are developed. The family has its presence across India and abroad as well.
lettersTitle: A few selected blessings from appreciation letters...

logoDescription.title: ...The secret of the Adhyatm Parivar logo adorned with the seven sacred elements...

items.title: 1. Jin Idol
items.desc: A replica of the supreme being of this world who shows the path to eternal and complete happiness.

items.title: 2. Jain Temple
items.desc: The most sacred place of that supreme being!

items.title: 3. Jain Scriptures
items.desc: Complete knowledge and the map of the path to attain true happiness!!

items.title: 4-5. Shraman–Shramani (Ogha)
items.desc: Those who diligently walk on this path themselves according to this knowledge and map, and show this true path to the world!

items.title: 6-7. Shravak–Shravika (Charvala)
items.desc: Those who are devoted to the followers of this path, who aspire to walk on this path and dedicate their body, mind, and wealth for it!

items.title: Bull
items.desc: The emblem of Shri Adinath Parmatma, the first Tirthankar and founder of the Shasan in Bharat Kshetra!

items.title: Lion
items.desc: The emblem of Shri Mahavir Parmatma, the last Tirthankar and reformer of the Shasan in Bharat Kshetra!

items.title: Dharmachakra
items.desc: The 24 spokes of the Dharmachakra remind us of the 24 Tirthankars of this Avasarpini era!

items.title: Lotus
items.desc: A symbol of the pure and detached state to be attained through devotion and protection of the seven sacred elements!

symbolicLogo.title: Due to Ashatana or other reasons
symbolicLogo.desc: Wherever the main logo cannot be used, in such situations this symbolic logo of Adhyatm Parivar is used instead.

vibhag.title: Jin Idol Department
vibhag.description: Scientific and traditional methods such as coating, polishing, etc. for the protection of all Jin idols across India from ancient to modern times. Creation of new beautiful stone and metal idols, removal of idol-related defects, etc.

vibhag.title: Jin Temple Department
vibhag.description: Resolving issues such as leakage problems in Jain temples across India, annual purification wherever possible, craftsmanship support for new temple construction, etc.

vibhag.title: Jinagam Department
vibhag.description: Cataloging Jain manuscripts located across India and abroad, editing more than 1.5 crore verses, approximately 15,000 ancient Jain texts, and publishing various books.

vibhag.title: Adhyatm Department
vibhag.description: Researching and implementing solutions to resolve obstacles faced by the four pillars of Jain Shasan — monks, nuns, laymen, and laywomen — in their spiritual practices. Around 18 to 20 types of activities are currently ongoing in this department.

sba.title: An Offering of Devotion to the Shasan
sba.subtitle: A glimpse of the service and protection activities carried out by Adhyatm Parivar
sba.comingSoon: New photos will be added soon

letters.quote: Applying tika etc. for the protection of idols is extremely risky, and the craftsmen currently working on polishing etc. are not completely reliable. Therefore, it is beneficial to carry out such work with the support of Adhyatm Parivar.
letters.author: Babu Amichand Pannalal

letters.quote: In the present time, pure and disciplined saints find it extremely rare to get pure land in cities. Your family has taken the initiative to create artificial pure land for them to protect the Shasan from decline. Countless thanks to Adhyatm Parivar for this effort...
letters.author: Dharmalabh of Muni Jagatshekharvijay

letters.quote: Our Shri Sangh requested the protection of such a Jin temple which is a heritage of the Shasan, and your family accepted it. Great appreciation to Shri Adhyatm Parivar.
letters.author: Shri Shwetambar Murtipujak Tapagachchh

letters.quote: Our society used to receive frequent complaints regarding drainage issues, but this time with your guidance the problem has been resolved. Complaints have almost stopped. I request all Sanghs to construct drainage in the same way.
letters.author: Shri Shwetambar Murtipujak Tapagachchh

letters.quote: Just as the Ganga flows from Gangotri and spreads across a vast land, may your family’s scriptural journey also expand widely. Once again, heartfelt appreciation for your work.
letters.author: Shraman Bhagwant

letters.quote: Many rare and unpublished ancient scriptures were researched, edited, and published for the first time by the revered ones. Great appreciation for this work.
letters.author: Su. Babubhai Saremall, Bedawala

letters.quote: For a long time during the monsoon season, rainwater used to leak from the roof of our Shri Chintamani Parshvanath Jinalaya. After contacting you, the work was completed beautifully. Now there is no leakage at all, and we have been saved from Ashatana of the temple.
letters.author: Shri Indrapuri Jain Sangh, Vadodara

letters.quote: From Dungarpur (Raj.), Shri Adinathji Jain Sangh received brass temple equipment sent by you for our Jain temple. Our Sangh sincerely appreciates your support.
letters.author: Shri Sambhavnathji Jain Shwetambar Murtipujak

letters.quote: In our Shri Sangh, we received a water stand through you so that in the Ayambilshala and for revered monks and nuns, hot water can cool down quickly. Shri Sangh deeply appreciates your noble work.
letters.author: Shri Rander Road Jain Sangh, Surat
